agents.name: Rex
agents.note:
Session 138 -- US-266 SHIPPED passes:true (Sprint 22 discriminator B: tick() silent-bail audit + DEBUG instrumentation). TWO artifacts: src/pi/power/orchestrator.py UPDATE (DEBUG-log 4 silent-bail early-returns -- thresholds.enabled=False / state==TRIGGER terminal / source==EXTERNAL during NORMAL state Guard 3 path B / source!=BATTERY UNKNOWN -- each capturing bail-causing value via `getattr(currentSource, 'value', currentSource)` plus currentVcell; `tick early-return:` prefix matches journalctl `grep` ergonomics for post-Drain-7 forensic walk; mod-history entry; ~50 lines net), tests/pi/power/test_tick_gating_no_silent_bail.py NEW (5 tests across 2 classes: TestSilentBailEarlyReturnsLog -- 4 parametrized discriminator tests one-per-silent-bail-guard each asserting `len(bailMessages) == 1` AND bail-causing value in message AND no state transition AND `tickCount` advanced by exactly 1 [US-262 invariant proof]; TestHappyPathEmitsNoBailLog -- 1 negative-invariant test that runs a real BATTERY drain crossing WARNING and asserts ZERO `tick early-return` DEBUG lines so the discriminator only fires when the bug class actually exists; ~430 lines). AUDIT FINDING: spec's `vcell=None` and `threshold=None` hypothesis modes were AUDITED and REJECTED as non-silent -- both currently TypeError on threshold comparison and are caught loud by hardware_manager._powerDownTickLoop's except-Exception handler (ERROR log, not silent bail). Per the behavior-unchanged invariant NO new defensive guards were added; spec test names re-mapped to actual silent-bail guards in test docstrings. Verification: tests/pi/power/test_tick_gating_no_silent_bail.py 5/5 PASS in 51.78s; pi/power + pi/hardware + pi/diagnostics + pi/integration subset 363 passed / 11 skipped / 0 failures in 124.32s (zero regressions; this proves US-266 did not perturb US-265's tick-thread health-check tests or any orchestrator state-machine tests). Runtime-validation gate: temporarily reverted DEBUG logs in orchestrator.py, re-ran 5 tests, confirmed 4 discriminator tests FAILED with `bailMessages == []` and happy-path negative-invariant still PASSED, then restored DEBUG logs and re-confirmed 5/5 pass. Ruff clean (one auto-fix for I001 import-sort on the new test file). sprint_lint US-266 OK. Discriminator-B now fully wired alongside US-265's discriminator-A liveness probe: post-Drain-7 a `journalctl | grep 'tick early-return:'` will name which guard (if any) swallowed the BATTERY -> WARNING transition; combined with logger CSV's pd_tick_count + pd_stage columns the truth-table is now fully observable per Spool's spec. Pattern carryforward: paired-message log structure (every bail log includes BOTH the bail-causing value AND irrelevant-but-correlatable values like vcell+source) lets a post-mortem tie the orchestrator-level bail to the simultaneous drain_forensics CSV row at the (timestamp, pd_tick_count, pd_stage, vcell_v) level. Sprint 22 status now: 5/8 shipped (US-262 + US-263 + US-264 + US-265 + US-266); remaining 3 unclaimed -- US-267 (S, P0; discriminator C = stage-row fsync + audit error propagation -- the natural NEXT pick because US-265 + US-266 + US-267 form Spool's discriminator trio and Drain-7's truth-table verdict needs all three lit) + US-268 (M, P0; TD-042 mechanical theme-stub addition across 4 test files; closes 24 TD-042 failures + brings fast suite to fully-green) + US-269 (S, P0; TD-044 one-liner SemVer-shape regex). Recommended next-iteration pick: US-267 (closes the discriminator trio so Drain Test 7's CSV will fully discriminate which hypothesis was the bug; alternative US-268 if a quick test-baseline win is preferred since the discriminator trio is independent of the TD-042 baseline).

----- PRIOR SESSION 137 -----
Session 137 -- US-265 SHIPPED passes:true (Sprint 22 discriminator A: tick-thread liveness instrumentation). FOUR artifacts: src/pi/hardware/hardware_manager.py UPDATE (+tickHealthCheckIntervalS constructor parameter default 60.0; +5 state fields _lastTickHealthCheckMono/_lastTickHealthCheckCount/_lastTickHealthCheckOnBattery/_tickHealthAlarmCount + factory wiring; +tickHealthAlarmCount read-only property; +_checkTickThreadHealth helper that snapshots orchestrator.tickCount on a configurable cadence and logs ERROR + increments alarm counter when count unchanged across full window while on BATTERY; loop entry now logs `tid=<id>` + intervalS at INFO; per-iteration health-check call wrapped in try/except so health-check failure CANNOT halt the tick loop per invariant; thread spawn-site log enriched with daemon + ident; factory reads pi.power.tickHealthCheckIntervalS config; mod-history entry; +time module import; ~135 lines net), src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/lifecycle.py UPDATE (audit log line in _wirePowerDownOrchestratorCallbacks confirming the orchestrator was instantiated by HardwareManager and the dedicated tick thread spawn is delegated to HardwareManager._startComponents -- pairs symmetrically with hardware_manager `tid=<id>` so a post-Drain-7 forensic walk can prove which side of the boundary failed if the spawn line is missing in journalctl; mod-history entry; ~20 lines net), tests/pi/hardware/test_power_down_tick_thread_health.py NEW (15 tests across 4 classes -- helper-direct discriminator scenarios + property exposure + constructor-param defaults + tick-loop integration including health-check-raising-does-not-kill-loop invariant; ALL 15 would FAIL against pre-fix code per runtime-validation gate -- AttributeError on missing _checkTickThreadHealth + tickHealthAlarmCount, TypeError on missing tickHealthCheckIntervalS kwarg; ~430 lines), tests/pi/power/test_staged_shutdown_actually_fires.py UPDATE (scope-extension: 5-line state-stub addition x3 test methods that bypass __init__ via __new__ and only set bare-minimum attributes the loop reads; my tickHealthCheckIntervalS state addition broke them on the entry log; this is a direct-consequence test update of an API change, not tangential drift -- documented inline). Verification: tests/pi/hardware/test_power_down_tick_thread_health.py 15/15 PASS in 21.87s; pi/{hardware,power,orchestrator,diagnostics,integration} subset 452 passed / 11 skipped / 0 failures in 149.68s (pre-touch was 437 + 3 staged-shutdown regressions; +15 new + 0 regressions); full fast suite 3833 passed / 26 failed / 17 skipped / 19 deselected in 1006.30s (16:46) -- baseline (Session 136 per US-264) was 3818 pass / 26 fail, delta +15 = exactly the new test count + ZERO regressions; the 26 failures unchanged TD-042 + TD-044 set (closed by US-268 + US-269 later this sprint). Ruff clean across all 4 touched files. sprint_lint US-265 OK (only carryover warning is US-263 sizing). Important runtime-validation note: discriminator test test_loopOnBatteryWithNoopTick_raisesAlarm mocks orchestrator.tick at the right level per Sprint 19 lesson (Mock with no side_effect so tickCount stays 0); a no-op tick simulates exactly the hypothesis-A pathology -- thread runs the loop body but counter never advances. Pre-Drain-7 this discriminator surface is now fully wired: when CIO runs Drain Test 7 with US-262 logger active, the journalctl ERROR line `PowerDownOrchestrator tick liveness alarm: tickCount=N unchanged across X.Xs while on BATTERY` will fire 60s after the first health-check window if hypothesis A is the bug; concurrent with the logger CSV's pd_tick_count column staying at 0. If that line does NOT appear in journalctl AND pd_tick_count IS incrementing in the CSV, hypothesis A is RULED OUT and the bug is hypothesis B (US-266) or C (US-267). Pattern carryforward: paired-3-way-symmetry log strategy (lifecycle audit + spawn-site daemon+ident + loop entry tid) lets a post-incident forensic walk in journalctl prove EXACTLY which side of the boundary failed -- reusable for any future cross-module thread-startup instrumentation. Sprint 22 status now: 4/8 shipped (US-262 + US-263 + US-264 + US-265); remaining 4 unclaimed -- US-266 + US-267 discriminator pair (M/S; the other two hypothesis fixes; runtime-validation gate critical for Drain-7 truth-table) + US-268 TD-042 (M; mechanical theme-stub addition across 4 test files; closes 24 TD-042 failures) + US-269 TD-044 (S; one-liner SemVer-shape regex on test_migration_0005). Next-iteration recommended pick: US-266 (M; independent; the discriminator pair lands cleanly together since US-265 covers thread-liveness and US-266 covers gating-logic which are orthogonal in HardwareManager + PowerDownOrchestrator code paths); alternatively US-268 if a quick test-baseline win is preferred (closes the 24 TD-042 failures + brings fast suite to fully-green before discriminator trio completes).
